is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum infections treated with amodiaquine in Kenya.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfmdr1 N86Y genotyping: '15N, 40Y and 17 mixed N/Y genotypes on day 0' — codon 86 is a target marker. Study focus is pfmdr1 gene amplification (copy number), which is not a curated codon.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: pfmdr1 N86Y distribution given as counts: 15N, 40Y, 17 mixed of the day-0 samples, allowing extractable frequency.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from clinical trial conducted in Western Kenya, Siaya Hospital, Nyanza Province — a sub-national location below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Clinical trial conducted between November and December 2004 in Western Kenya — a single, well-bounded collection window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human field/clinical isolates from children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Western Kenya.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study on 72 children treated with amodiaquine monotherapy.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'Samples for molecular analysis were derived from our clinical trial on AQ in monotherapy conducted between November and December 2004 in Western Kenya (Holmgren et al. 2006)'; the N86Y data is explicitly from that prior publication: 'The pfmdr1 N86Y genotyping analysis is described in a previous report (Holmgren et al. 2006).'

supplement.evidence: N86Y counts are given in the main text; no supplementary pointers for additional per-locus data.

assessment.summary: Primary study of 72 children with P. falciparum malaria in Western Kenya (Siaya, Nyanza Province, Nov-Dec 2004) treated with amodiaquine monotherapy. The focus is pfmdr1 gene copy number/amplification (not a curated codon). It reports pfmdr1 N86Y genotype counts (15N, 40Y, 17 mixed on day 0), a target marker with extractable frequency, at sub-country resolution within a bounded time window. All criteria appear met, but the N86Y data derives from a prior publication (Holmgren et al. 2006), creating high duplicate risk.
exclusion_reasons: High duplicate risk: the pfmdr1 N86Y genotype data is explicitly taken from a previously published report (Holmgren et al. 2006) using the same 2004 Western Kenya samples; this paper's novel contribution is copy-number amplification, not a curated codon frequency.
